name: Seven Hanging Valleys Trail
region: Lagoa e Carvoeiro e Carvoeiro e Carvoeiro
description: Jog this moderate 7.3-mile (11.8 km) coastal route along the Sete Vales Suspensos, featuring cliffs, beaches, and the Alfanzina Lighthouse.
region_intro: Jogging routes around Lagoa e Carvoeiro offer a diverse landscape characterized by dramatic limestone cliffs, golden sandy beaches, and unique rock formations along the coastline. The region provides a variety of trails that combine physical activity with scenic views of the Atlantic Ocean. Runners can explore coastal paths, hidden coves, and areas with impressive natural arches and grottoes. The terrain ranges from relatively flat stretches to routes with moderate elevation changes, catering to different fitness levels. Best jogging routes around Lagoa e Carvoeiro The most popular jogging route is Seven Hanging Valleys Trail, a 7.3 miles (11.8 km) trail that takes 1 hour 25 minutes to complete. This route follows the coastline, offering views of hidden beaches and natural arches. Another top favourite among local runners is Benagil Caves – Marinha Beach loop from Praia de Benagil, a moderate 5.9 miles (9.5 km) path. This trail winds through coastal landscapes, providing glimpses of the famous Benagil Cave area and Marinha Beach. Local runners also love the Fontainhas Beach – Albandeira Arch loop from Forte e Capela de Nossa Senhora da Rocha, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail leading through coastal paths with views of rock formations, often completed in about 32 minutes. Jogging around Lagoa e Carvoeiro is defined by golden cliffs, turquoise waters, and unique rock formations, with options ranging from easy coastal paths to more challenging routes with varied elevation. The network offers options for different ability levels, including 4 easy, 21 moderate, and 7 difficult routes. The routes in Lagoa e Carvoeiro are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 500 runners have used komoot to explore Lagoa e Carvoeiro's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running routes are available in Lagoa e Carvoeiro?
A: There are over 30 dedicated running routes around Lagoa e Carvoeiro. These trails offer a diverse landscape, from dramatic limestone cliffs to golden sandy beaches, catering to various preferences and fitness levels.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the jogging trails in Lagoa e Carvoeiro?
A: The terrain is quite varied. You'll find coastal paths along dramatic limestone cliffs, golden sandy beaches, and unique rock formations. Some routes offer relatively flat stretches, while others, particularly around Carvoeiro towards Cavalho, feature hilly roads with significant inclines for a more challenging workout.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners or families in Lagoa e Carvoeiro?
A: Yes, there are several options for easier runs. The region offers 4 easy routes, perfect for beginners or families looking for a gentle jog. These often follow coastal paths, providing scenic views without demanding elevation changes.

Q: Can I find challenging running trails in the Lagoa e Carvoeiro area?
A: Absolutely. For those seeking a challenge, there are 7 difficult routes available. These trails often feature more significant elevation changes and varied terrain, such as the hilly roads around Carvoeiro, providing a robust workout amidst stunning scenery.

Q: What do other runners enjoy most about jogging in Lagoa e Carvoeiro?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 28 reviews. Runners frequently praise the breathtaking coastal scenery, the well-preserved natural beauty, and the diverse trails that combine physical activity with spectacular views of the Atlantic Ocean, hidden beaches, and unique rock formations.

Q: Are there any circular running paths in Lagoa e Carvoeiro?
A: Yes, many of the routes in Lagoa e Carvoeiro are designed as loops, offering convenient circular paths. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19325886" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benagil Caves – Marinha Beach loop from Praia de Benagil&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 5.9-mile (9.5 km) path that winds through coastal landscapes.

Q: What landmarks or points of interest can I see along the running routes?
A: The routes offer numerous stunning landmarks. Along the coastal trails, you can spot the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/867457" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Benagil Caves&lt;/a&gt;, the beautiful &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/190198" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Carvalho Beach&lt;/a&gt;, and the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/165055" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Algar Seco Rock Formations&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1422327687/seven-hanging-valleys-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Seven Hanging Valleys Trail&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, offers views of hidden beaches, natural arches, and sea caves.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly running routes in Lagoa e Carvoeiro?
A: While specific dog-friendly designations vary, many of the natural coastal paths and trails are suitable for running with dogs, provided they are kept on a leash and you adhere to local regulations regarding pet access to beaches and protected areas. Always check local signage.

Q: What is the best time of year to go running in Lagoa e Carvoeiro?
A: The region is excellent for running year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures, ideal for longer runs. Even in winter, the mild climate makes it suitable for outdoor activities. Summers can be hot, so early mornings or late evenings are recommended during peak season to avoid the midday sun.

Q: Are there any less crowded running spots or hidden gems in Lagoa e Carvoeiro?
A: While popular trails like the Seven Hanging Valleys can get busy, exploring routes slightly off the main tourist paths can reveal quieter spots. The region's commitment to preserving its natural beauty means many areas remain relatively untouched, offering serene environments for running and enjoying nature walks away from the crowds.

Q: Are there running routes accessible by public transport in Lagoa e Carvoeiro?
A: Many of the popular trailheads are located near towns or villages that have public transport connections. For example, routes starting near Praia de Benagil or Marinha Beach may be accessible via local bus services. It's advisable to check local bus schedules and routes for specific starting points.

Q: Where can I find parking near the running trails in Lagoa e Carvoeiro?
A: Parking is generally available near popular trailheads and beaches, such as Praia da Marinha or Praia de Benagil. However, during peak season, these areas can fill up quickly. It's recommended to arrive early or explore routes that start from less congested areas to find easier parking.

Q: Can I combine running with exploring other attractions in the area?
A: Absolutely. The region is rich in attractions. After a run, you could explore nearby historical sites like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/326759" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Silves Castle&lt;/a&gt; or visit other natural wonders. Many running routes pass close to stunning beaches and viewpoints, allowing you to easily extend your outing.
tips: Unique beach with exciting rock formations

The Alfanzina Lighthouse built in 1920 on a rocky promontory. It is a white quadrangular tower with a red lantern, important for navigation. The lighthouse is accessible to the public on Wednesdays. Its facilities were expanded in 1952 and connected to the electricity grid in 1980.

Praia do Vale de Centeanes, located near Carvoeiro, is famous for its natural beauty and proximity to the Benagil cave and is accessible to people with reduced mobility. The beach is sheltered by golden cliffs, offering a peaceful environment. It is also the starting point of the Seven Hanging Valleys trail. The beach is guarded, ideal for families and has a restaurant with sea views.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like for jogging on the Sete Vales Suspensos — circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is primarily natural, uneven, and rocky, following clifftops. You should expect several climbs and descents, some of which are steep. Sturdy footwear is essential due to the uneven ground and occasional sections that may require some scrambling.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along this route?
faqs.answer: The route offers breathtaking panoramic views of turquoise waters, secluded beaches, and unique geological formations. Key highlights include the iconic Benagil Cave (viewable from above), the stunning Marinha Beach with its spectacular rock arches, and the picturesque Vale de Centeanes Beach. You'll also pass the Farol de Alfanzina lighthouse and hidden coves like Praia do Carvalho, accessible via a carved tunnel.

faqs.question: Is the Sete Vales Suspensos — circular route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Information regarding specific dog rules for this trail is not explicitly provided in the available data. However, as a natural coastal path, it's generally advisable to keep dogs on a leash to protect wildlife and for their safety near cliffs. Always check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to jog this route?
faqs.answer: The trail is popular year-round, but for jogging, spring and autumn offer more comfortable temperatures. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon runs preferable. Winters are generally mild but can bring rain.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this trail?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to access the Sete Vales Suspensos trail itself. It is a public coastal path.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Sete Vales Suspensos trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be accessed from various points. Popular starting points with parking include areas near Marinha Beach or Vale de Centeanes Beach. Parking availability can vary, especially during peak season, so arriving early is recommended.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to jog the Sete Vales Suspensos — circular route?
faqs.answer: For jogging, this route typically takes around 1 hour and 25 minutes, depending on your fitness level and how often you stop to enjoy the views. The total distance is approximately 11.8 km (7.3 miles).

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for beginner joggers?
faqs.answer: This route is classified as 'moderate' for jogging and requires good fitness due to its uneven, rocky terrain and steep sections. While not impossible for beginners, it's more suitable for those with some trail running experience or a good level of physical conditioning.

faqs.question: How can I reach the Sete Vales Suspensos trail by public transport?
faqs.answer: Public transport options to the trailheads, such as Praia da Marinha or Praia de Vale Centeanes, are available. For detailed schedules and routes, it's best to consult local public transport information or websites like visitalgarve.pt or algarveportugaltourism.com for up-to-date information, especially for weekend schedules.

faqs.question: What essential gear should I bring for jogging this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the rocky and uneven terrain, sturdy trail running shoes with good grip are essential. It's also advisable to carry enough water, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a phone for emergencies. Some sections are steep, so consider trekking poles if you prefer extra stability.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Sete Vales Suspensos — circular route fully encompasses the official 'Percurso dos Sete Vales Suspensos' trail. It also briefly intersects with sections of 'Rua dos Pescadores'.
